title: chenopodium album var. subaphyllum
intro: Chenopodium album var. subaphyllum, also known as lamb's quarters or goosefoot, is a variety of the Chenopodium album plant species, which belongs to the amaranth family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its high content of vitamins A and C, potassium, and other essential minerals, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Chenopodium album var. subaphyllum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the skin to reap its medicinal benefits.
